content.full_text:
TO: [ ] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[ ] LYNN        [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH        [ ] BOB S.
[ ] HELEN       [ ] OTHER BOB LEIDIGH and KATHRYN DONOVAN -- FYI

FROM: J. Pritchard
DATE: 12/26/84

[X] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: 82013; 82015; 82025

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [XX]

CALLER Mr. Vinabel, Treasurer for
REPRESENTING Ernie Fanner, Yolo County Supervisor
PHONE NUMBER:

QUESTION: A group which received donations has filed a lawsuit challenging Mr. Fanner's eligibility to serve as fifth district supervisor on the basis that he does not reside in the district. Mr. Vinabel asked whether the group qualified as a committee when it received $500 and, therefore, must file campaign statements.

ANSWER: NO. The money received and spent by the group is not for "political purposes" because it is not for the purpose of influencing the voters. The group does not qualify as a committee or incur reporting obligations under the campaign provisions.
